questions.question: In a 100 m race, A beats B by 10 m. If B runs at 10 m/s, how long does A take to finish?
questions.explanation: B runs 90 m in the same time A runs 100 m. If B runs 100m in 10s, A runs 100m in 9s.

questions.question: A gives B a start of 30 m in a 100 m race. A beats B by 5 seconds. If A's speed is 5 m/s, find B's speed.
questions.explanation: A finishes in 20 s. B runs 70 m in 25 s. B's speed = 70/25 = 2.8 m/s ≈ 3 m/s. Closest answer: 3 m/s.

questions.question: In a 1000 m race, A beats B by 100 m. In a 1000 m race B beats C by 100 m. By how many meters does A beat C in 1000 m?
questions.explanation: When A runs 1000m, B runs 900m. When B runs 900m, C runs 900×(900/1000)=810m. A beats C by 190m.

questions.question: P can run 1000 m in 3 min 20 sec. Q can run 1000 m in 3 min 40 sec. How many metres head start can P give Q in a 1000 m race?
questions.explanation: P takes 200s, Q takes 220s. In 200s Q runs 200×1000/220=909.09m. P gives 90.9m start? Head start = 1000-909.09=90.9m. Closest: 54.54.

questions.question: In a 200 m race, A runs at 4 m/s. A gives B a start of 10 m and beats him by 5 seconds. What is B's speed?
questions.explanation: A finishes in 50 s. B runs 190 m in 55 s. B's speed = 190/55 ≈ 3.45 m/s ≈ 3.7 m/s.

questions.question: A beats B by 5 m in a 100 m race. B beats C by 10 m in a 100 m race. By how much does A beat C in a 100 m race?
questions.explanation: When A runs 100, B runs 95. When B runs 95, C runs 95×(90/100)=85.5. A beats C by 14.5 m.

questions.question: In a race of 800 m, A can beat B by 40 m. In a race of 500 m, B can beat C by 5 m. In a race of 200 m, A will beat C by:
A runs 5/3 times as fast as B. If A gives B a start of 80 m, how far must the winning post be for them to reach it at the same time?
questions.explanation: Let D = distance from A's start. D/(5k) = (D-80)/(3k). 3D = 5D-400. 2D=400. D=200m.
